description:
You are helping a script-developer configure their shell environment.

Current situation:
1. A directory that will hold utility scripts already exists at  
   /home/user/scripts
2. The file /home/user/.bashrc is present but does NOT yet contain any reference to UTIL_SCRIPTS.

Goal (what the automated grader will verify):
A. The very last line of /home/user/.bashrc must become  
   export UTIL_SCRIPTS="/home/user/scripts"

   • There must be no leading or trailing spaces other than the single space that normally appears after the word “export”.  
   • The double quotation marks around the path are mandatory.  
   • The line must end with a single newline character (ASCII 0x0A).

B. A log file must be created at /home/user/task_completion.log containing exactly two lines and nothing else:

   Line 1: UTIL_SCRIPTS=/home/user/scripts  
   Line 2: status=done  

   • Each line must terminate with a newline character.  
   • No extra blank lines, spaces, or carriage-returns are allowed.

Constraint:
Achieve both Goal A and Goal B with ONE single shell command executed in the terminal (a command chain using “&&” or “;” still counts as one command because you will press Enter only once).

Reminder:
Do not output the command itself here—perform the work directly in the terminal session. When finished, the grader will open /home/user/.bashrc and /home/user/task_completion.log to check that they match the exact formats described above.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:
1. Directory /home/user/scripts already exists and is empty.
2. File /home/user/.bashrc exists with the following content and newline at the end:
   # .bashrc autogenerated for training
   # feel free to add new exports below this line

Expected final state after successful task completion:
1. /home/user/.bashrc content will be:
   # .bashrc autogenerated for training
   # feel free to add new exports below this line
   export UTIL_SCRIPTS="/home/user/scripts"

2. /home/user/task_completion.log will exist with exact byte content (including trailing newlines):
   UTIL_SCRIPTS=/home/user/scripts\n
   status=done\n
